expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid banding scenarios distinguish styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes
expectations.evidence: The result explicitly names the case focus and enumerates the required facets: “explicit coverage of modern grid banding scenarios that distinguish styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes” and later lists implied Phase 4a subcategory scenarios including “interactions… [and] backward-compatible rendering outcomes.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The output is framed as a Phase 4a contract evaluation and checks Phase 4a-required artifacts/properties (e.g., “Expected artifacts per contract (Phase 4a)” and “Required properties to verify (from references/phase4a-contract.md): subcategory-only structure…”). It does not provide a Phase 4a draft, but the benchmark output itself aligns to the Phase 4a phase_contract assessment.
